title: Pooh
content:
{{Top|rwp}}
[[File:PoohtheBear.jpg|thumb|Pooh in his pen during recording.]]
'''Pooh''' was a young [[Wikipedia:cinnamon bear|cinnamon bear]] whose vocal sounds [[Ben Burtt]] used as the basis of [[Chewbacca]]'s voice.
==Biography==
Pooh was young by [[1975]], and was cared for by trainer [https://www.imdb.com/name/nm0185169/ Monty Cox] and his partner, stuntwoman [[Wikipedia:Susan Backlinie|Susan Backlinie]], in an animal farm near [[Wikipedia:Tehachapi, California|Tehachapi, California]].<ref name="guide">[[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']]</ref> 

While working for {{Film|IV}}, Ben Burtt was collecting sound samples of bears in order to create Chewbacca's speech. Having little luck with zoos, he visited Cox's farm with his boom technician Richard Anderson, and spent a day in Pooh's pen with the help of Backlinie. Pooh was deliberately unfed by Cox, and seeing his meal (bread soaked in milk) withheld, he produced crying and moaning sounds. During that process, Cox also paraded other animals near Pooh, such as a mountain lion on a leash, causing the young to produce different vocalized reactions. At one point Pooh stood up and hugged Anderson, who was terrified.<ref name="guide" />

Pooh's voice consisted the major bulk of Chewbacca's, and Burtt created a library of vocalizations, grouped according to the different emotions they conveyed according to their intonation -- anger, fear, happiness or inquiry. Burtt complemented the library with other sounds from walruses and dogs.<ref name="guide" />

For ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'' ([[1978]]), Burtt revisited Cox'x farm, but by that time Pooh had matured and was not cooperative.<ref name="guide" />
